name: A priest after the order of Melchizedek
notes: Hebrews grounds its doctrine of Christ's eternal priesthood in two Old Testament texts. The first is the sworn oracle of Psalm 110:4, quoted verbatim four times (Heb 5:6, 6:20, 7:17, 7:21) as the proof that the Messiah is "a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec"; Heb 7:21 also takes up the psalm's preceding clause, "The LORD hath sworn and will not repent." The second is the narrative of Genesis 14:18-20, which Heb 7:1-4 recounts to show who Melchizedek was: king of Salem and priest of the most high God, who blessed Abram and received tithes of all. The shared core is the figure of Melchizedek as both king and priest, the blessing of Abraham, and the tithe. Genesis supplies the historical type and the psalm supplies the prophetic word, while Hebrews draws out the interpretation each contributes.
